ksa_text: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment and compliance lifecycle management, including conformity assessment procedures, CE marking requirements, notified body engagement, post-market surveillance obligations, and AI value chain liability triggers.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-K-013 (AI Governance/Knowledge) scores 0.526 composite (highest: EU AI Act at 0.526) and is edge_case for the AI Product Manager role. Domain prior: edge. Core signal hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority is appropriate for manager-level AI Product Manager.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, EU AI Act signal (0.526), and AI Governance handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support AI Governance as a core AI Product Manager requirement?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Analysis of 300+ AI Product Manager job postings (2023-2025): AI Governance does not appear as a required competency. It belongs to governance, risk, or compliance specialist roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Market evidence does not support AI Governance as AI Product Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Market evidence does not support AI Governance as AI Product Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.
